records.habitat: Alvar grasslands, bedrock shores, and open calcareous habitats.
records.reason: Rare native wild populations in Michigan; this entry is about naturally occurring populations, not ordinary garden chives.
records.field_marks: Round hollow onion-scented leaves from bulbs; pink to lilac flowers packed in an upright rounded umbel.
records.look_alikes: Garden chives and other onion-like Allium species. Habitat matters: the rare entry is the wild plant in the right natural setting.
records.care_note: Do not share exact spots from natural populations.

records.habitat: Rich northern woods, cool calcareous forest, and other mesic sites with base-rich soils.
records.reason: A localized northern sedge with limited Michigan distribution.
records.field_marks: Broad arching sedge leaves in clumps, with drooping seed heads on slender stalks; looks more graceful and open than many stiff woodland sedges.
records.look_alikes: Other broad-leaved woodland sedges, especially species of rich northern forest. Fruiting structure is the safer separator.
records.care_note: Best handled as a sedge for careful photography rather than collecting.
